title: Getting paid to save the world
display_title: Getting paid to save the world
wikitext:
[]: Getting paid to save the world             Quote Post by Shamonan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 1:49 pm How much do you think the Eva pilots get paid for their 'part time jobs' of saving humanity? No one working at NERV appears to be really wealthy, though I guess looks can be deceiving, and they all subsist on civil servants' salaries. Even Misato admits to being perpetually broke, and she's third in command of the most powerful force in the world. She might just throw whatever the UN gives her away on beer, though... Now, Rei owns nothing except for some pills, water, a lease on a one-room apartment, bloody bandages, broken glasses, and a school uniform, so she just might have the most savings of anyone.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayana-Me » Sun Apr 15, 2007 2:13 pm In episode 21, Fuyutski criticizes Gendo for his apparently large salary at NERV (or was it GEHIRN at that point?), implying that he is possibly involved in some sort of fraudulent activity. It seems that beyond that, the pay is similar to that of any other military entity (rock bottom). As for the childrens' earnings, they probably don't make any money themselves due to their young age. Perhaps their wages go directly to their guardians, just like Malcolm in the Middle.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Sun Apr 15, 2007 2:44 pm I think that money that Fuyutsuki mentions were funds provided by Seele given to Gendo directly since project E was a rather secret project that doesn't need the attention of the media and stuff. As for salaries, most of the characters look to be badly paid. They mostly want the job because they get to be involved in the noble work of saving mankind.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 15, 2007 2:54 pm See also http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=2742

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Apr 15, 2007 3:17 pm You know Gendos probably rolling in big bucks, but everyone else is poor, Gendo stole most of everyones salary :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Apr 15, 2007 3:25 pm But Gendo only wants Yui back, and the best way to do that is to put as much money into Project E as possible. He's probably the poorest of the lot.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Apr 15, 2007 5:19 pm http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=67911#67911

[]:             Quote Post by Shamonan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 5:25 pm True, you don't ever see where Gendo lives, or him out of uniform or without a lab coat at NERV or GEHIRN, though Rei would know, since she's lived with him longer than Shinji has. Come to think of it, when Fuyutsuki first met Gendo in 1999, I was kind of surprised that he had been arrested for starting a bar fight. That's not what I expected from the Gendo Ikari of 2015, though he was probably a different man before Yui died.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Sun Apr 15, 2007 6:26 pm Rei wasn't really living with Gendo, dude. I'd like to bring the budget apartment to your attention.

[]:             Quote Post by Shamonan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 6:29 pm No, no; she lived with him until entering the seventh grade at Tokyo-3's middle school. Then she started living on her own (probably paid for by Gendo).

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Sun Apr 15, 2007 6:31 pm I don't remember much being said about her living arrangements except for that she just kind of showed up in seventh grade or so and didn't make any friends because she was antisocial. Source?

[]:             Quote Post by Shamonan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 7:00 pm Episode 21: a younger Rei, who is the 'daughter of a colleague' is staying with Gendo. She doesn't have any parents, so who else would she live with?

[]:             Quote Post by Kyle » Sun Apr 15, 2007 8:40 pm I know one thing-If im piloting a giant biorobo,risking my life,putting up with asukas bitching,misatos teasing,and countless other things id better be getting paid like a motherfucker. A guy who can't sing for shit gets paid millions of dollers to get on a stage and yell "bitches and hos" for two hours when our police forces and firefighters and others that are in a profesion that causes them to risk there lives on a daily bases and lose time with there family's make barely enough to pay the bills. Shit like that is the reason why i lost my faith in humanity. I don't know about you but i think that's fucked up. I'v said my piece.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Apr 15, 2007 9:30 pm I didn't think the kids even got paid. They just did it because it was something that had to be done, or everyone on Earth would die.

[]:             Quote Post by Kyle » Sun Apr 15, 2007 10:33 pm Guilt...thats all it was...guilt.

[]:             Quote Post by kaworu_nagisa » Sun Apr 15, 2007 11:35 pm Judging by the car Mistato drives in episode 1 and volume 1, I'd say around 90k a year. As for the Eva Pilots, I'm guessing about 500 per angel.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Apr 15, 2007 11:50 pm kaworu_nagisa wrote: Judging by the car Mistato drives

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Apr 15, 2007 11:58 pm 500 per angel

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Apr 16, 2007 12:07 am Defectron wrote: 500 per angel
